name: w3-project-memory
title: W3: Project memory — rolling projectBrief (W3a) + member expertiseTags write-back learning (W3b)
description: Two-part 'project gets smarter over time' memory feature. W3a: a rolling per-repo project brief at repos/{repoId}/meta/projectBrief { content (<=~500 words/3500 chars markdown), updatedAt, version }. Written best-effort at the END of summarizeDayFlow (after the report persists) by one extra MODELS.fast merge-summarize call (old brief + today's report -> new brief, evict stale info, hard length cap, version++). Read via a new pure helper tools/projectBrief.ts (readProjectBrief + formatBriefForPrompt), prepended as a stable cache-friendly context prefix in breakdownTaskFlow, assignTaskFlow, generateHandoffFlow, dailyBriefChatFlow. Empty brief => zero behavior change. W3b: assignTaskFlow's finalizeAssignment tool gains learnedTags?: string[] (agent derives from retrieved commit evidence); on finalize, merge into users/{userId}.expertiseTags (arrayUnion via set merge, cap total at 8, best-effort). readTeamState already surfaces expertiseTags, so the next assignment reads them back.
creator: gitsync
notes: PLANNING ONLY. Baseline verified in worktree: typecheck 0 errors, jest 33 suites / 250 tests. KEY REALITY-vs-SPEC discrepancy: expertiseTags live on the TOP-LEVEL users doc (apps/gitsync/users/{userId}.expertiseTags), NOT repos/{repoId}/members/{userId} as FINAL_DEMO_PLAN.md W3b states. readTeamState joins members(workload)+users(identity+expertise); the write-back MUST target users/{userId} with set+merge (the users doc may not pre-exist for a member). Awaiting Fable 5 review before any .ts is written.
